reasoning: The market estimate is 6% as of mid-October 2025, and the resolution date is Jan 1, 2026. With only slightly more than two months remaining, a 6% probability reflects a low but elevated risk compared to historical base rates. Iran has threatened to close the Strait for decades but has never fully done so, as it would severely harm its own economy and invite massive military retaliation. While the resolution criteria allow for 'severe restrictions' or interdiction of Western shipping (a lower bar than totally closing the Strait), this would still require a major escalation. Anchoring to the recent 6% market forecast is optimal given the brief ~10-week remaining timeframe until the end of 2025.
